title: Pad Thai

overview.description: Thailand's famous stir-fried rice noodle dish with chicken, egg, beansprouts and a balanced tamarind sauce.

method: Soak the rice noodles in warm water for 30–40 minutes until flexible but not fully soft.
Mix together the tamarind, fish sauce, palm sugar and soy sauce until the sugar has dissolved.
Heat a wok over a high heat.
Cook the chicken until just cooked through, then remove.
Cook the shallots and garlic for 30 seconds.
Push everything to one side and scramble the eggs.
Toss until the noodles absorb the sauce and become tender.
Return the chicken to the wok.
Add the beansprouts, spring onions and half the peanuts.
Serve immediately with the remaining peanuts, lime wedges, fresh beansprouts and chilli flakes.

learning.did-you-know: Pad Thai was promoted by the Thai government in the 1930s as part of a campaign to create a national dish.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to balance sweet, sour and salty flavours while stir-frying noodles over high heat.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: An authentic Thai favourite that uses ingredients already stocked in the Chapman Kitchen and cooks in minutes.
chapman-tips: Don't over-soak the noodles—they finish cooking in the wok.
Have every ingredient prepared before turning on the heat.
Taste before serving. The ideal balance is salty, sour and gently sweet.
Cook in batches if your wok is small to maintain high heat.
notes.leftovers: Reheat quickly in a hot wok, but the noodles are at their best immediately after cooking.
